prompt: Create a looping idle animation for a black widow spider model. The spider must be standing upright on all eight legs in a wide, stable stance — NOT collapsed, NOT face-down, NOT in a T-pose. The legs should be bent at the joints with feet planted on the ground plane, body raised above the ground. Subtle idle motion: the entire body gently rises and falls by about 2-3% of body height, like slow breathing. The front pair of legs (L1, R1) slightly lift and tap back down in an alternating rhythm, as if sensing the environment. The abdomen has a very gentle side-to-side sway. All motion is subtle and slow — the spider is alert and waiting, not twitching. The default standing height should place the body center roughly at 1/3 of the total leg span above the ground. Legs should be angled outward like a real spider's resting stance. Authored in model space (belly to +Y).
